title: OpenAI API
html:
OpenAI API

The first AI infrastructure Yahir actually shipped against — both as a witnessing event (May 2024) and as a production dependency (Dec 2024). The two appearances frame the gap in which Yahir converts from AI-watcher to AI-builder.

The witnessing moment is in 2024 05 13 things, May 13 2024, the day OpenAI announced GPT-4o (the voice-mode launch). Yahir, in the middle of the OSU finals push and the Shanghai study abroad visa scramble, writes one line in his running todo: "OpenAI announcement — This is appearing to be a monumental moment that may decide the future of what I do, idk??" He flags it as the fork in his career path, then goes back to his ALGOS midterm. The line is doing two things at once — Yahir naming the moment correctly and deferring action because he's mid-finals. The double-take is itself characteristic.

By December 2024 (2024 12 21 todos) the OpenAI API is infrastructure. The BroMenu product spec — written in late December 2024 right after departing China — names the OpenAI Camera API for OCR of restaurant menus and the GPT API for anabolic scoring of menu items, paired with Supabase for storage and SwiftUI in WindSurf IDE for the frontend. The relevant constraint from the same note: "Reduce API call costs by caching responses wherever possible" — no runway discipline showing up at the technical-architecture layer. Yahir is building production AI 7 months after he first flagged the May 2024 announcement.

The API also matters in the Rankett arc as the implicit substrate. The 2026 01 07 rankett meetup prep guide never names individual model APIs explicitly because Rankett's value-prop is across AI assistants (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, Grok, DeepSeek) — but the audit-and-rescore loop that powers the AI visibility engine has to query each one's API surface. OpenAI is in the bundle.

The privacy footnote from 2024 12 21 todos is worth keeping: the raw BroMenu spec ends with three credentials — an OPENAI_API_KEY (looks intact), a SUPABASE_URL, and a SUPABASE_KEY (already redacted). The OpenAI key in the raw file may not have been redacted; flagged for rotation.
